title: node-postgres Pool, Client Ownership, Transactions, and Shutdown · Inference Brief: Feed Ts Pool Lifecycle
summary: Each request creates a pool, transaction statements call pool.query, checked-out clients leak on error, row values are trusted, and process exit races pool timers. Predict the reader decision, external-unknown boundary, Promise and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL transaction, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed ts pool lifecycle increment in bounded pg repository and transaction port. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Create one bounded Pool, handle background errors, observe acquire waits, reject work during closure, and await pool.end after workers settle.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Create one bounded Pool, handle background errors, observe acquire waits, reject work during closure, and await pool.end after workers settle.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Pool Lifecycle model · Theory Feed Ts Pool Lifec…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Pool Lifecycle precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Pool Lifecycle model · Theory Feed Ts Pool Lifec…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 1271 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Pool, checked-out client, transaction connection, row validation, error, and close ownership; every external value must remain unknown until current runtime validation admits it; then change one tenant, cursor, reader command, authorization, validator, sanitizer, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Create one bounded Pool, handle background errors, observe acquire waits, reject work during closure, and await pool.end after workers settle.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Pool Lifecycle model · Theory Feed Ts Pool Lifec…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A pool per handler isolates transactions and frees connections sooner.
